p1|ECO.summary: Satisfaction with the country's current economic situation (ordinal scale).

p2|ECO.summary: Personal satisfaction with one's own current economic situation (ordinal scale).

p3|ECO.summary: Perceived economic living standard compared to parents at respondent's current age (comparative scale).

p4|ECO.summary: Belief about children's future economic living standard compared to respondent (comparative scale).

p5|ECO.summary: Attribution of responsibility for the country's economic situation (open-ended).

p6_1|ECO.summary: Perceived impact of insecurity on the country's economic advancement (ordinal scale).

p7_1|ECO.summary: Reasons for Mexican emigration to the United States (first mention, open-ended).

p8|ECO.summary: Reasons for willingness to live outside Mexico (open-ended).

p9_1|ECO.summary: Government priority for the economy: preventing price increases (ranked importance, first mention).

p10_1|ECO.summary: Perceived importance of economic situation as a cause of unemployment (ordinal scale).

p11_1|ECO.summary: Perceived effectiveness of education and professional guidance in creating employment (ordinal scale).

p12|ECO.summary: Assessment of the 2012 labor reform's impact on employment in the country (ordinal scale).

p13|ECO.summary: Perceived reasons for high informal employment in Mexico (open-ended).

p14|ECO.summary: Willingness to take action if government corruption were eliminated (open-ended).

p15_1|ECO.summary: Agreement with statement that national companies are more competitive and efficient due to NAFTA (ordinal scale).

p16_3|ECO.summary: Adequacy of minimum wage to cover education needs (binary scale).

p18_2|ECO.summary: Importance of high income when evaluating a job (ordinal scale).

p19|ECO.summary: Most valued job characteristic among secondary options (ranking/selection).

p20|ECO.summary: Most important job characteristic among final set of options (ranking/selection).

p21|ECO.summary: Employment preference regarding contract type and work arrangement (ordinal/categorical).

p22|ECO.summary: Perception of worker rights respect in Mexico (ordinal scale).

p23|ECO.summary: Perceived necessity of labor unions (ordinal scale).

p24|ECO.summary: Opinion about labor unions' role or characteristics (open-ended/ordinal).

p25|ECO.summary: Current employment status (categorical: employed, unemployed, inactive, etc.).

p28|ECO.summary: Number of employees at respondent's workplace (numeric/categorical).

p31|ECO.summary: Work arrangement type (self-employed, employee, family worker, etc.).

p32|ECO.summary: Professional status or position in current job (categorical).

p33|ECO.summary: Employment relationship: direct hire by employer or through intermediary company (binary).

p34|ECO.summary: Type of labor contract or employment relationship (categorical).

p35|ECO.summary: Reasons for maintaining temporary employment contract (open-ended).

p36|ECO.summary: Type of work schedule (full-time, part-time, shift work, etc.).

p40|ECO.summary: Payment for overtime hours worked in the past week (binary).

p41|ECO.summary: Number of Saturdays worked in the past month (numeric).

p42|ECO.summary: Number of Sundays worked in the past month (numeric).

p44|ECO.summary: Reasons for not contributing to social security (open-ended).

p45_1|ECO.summary: Self-assessment of income compared to other workers in same sector (ordinal scale).

p46_1|ECO.summary: Job satisfaction regarding opportunities for advancement or promotion (ordinal scale).

p48|ECO.summary: Perceived probability of job loss in the next twelve months (ordinal scale).

p51_1|ECO.summary: Primary reason for wanting to change jobs (first mention, open-ended).

p52|ECO.summary: Presence of secondary paid employment or family work (binary).

p53|ECO.summary: Weekly hours worked in secondary employment (numeric).

p54|ECO.summary: Quality or assessment of secondary employment (ordinal/categorical).

p55|ECO.summary: Other economic activities performed in the past month (categorical/multiple response).

p56|ECO.summary: Job search activity in the past three months (binary).

p56a|ECO.summary: Reasons for not searching for employment (open-ended).

p57|ECO.summary: Expected duration of job search in months (numeric).

p58|ECO.summary: Perceived probability of finding employment in the next twelve months (ordinal scale).

p59_1|ECO.summary: Willingness to accept job requiring change of residence (binary).

p59_3|ECO.summary: Willingness to accept job with lower income than considered appropriate (binary).

p59_4|ECO.summary: Willingness to accept job with lower professional status than current (binary).

p60|ECO.summary: Ability to start work or self-employment within one month if offered (binary).

p61|ECO.summary: Employment status in first job ever held (categorical).

p63|ECO.summary: Occupation or job description in first job ever held (open-ended).

p64|ECO.summary: Educational level when starting first job (categorical).

p65|ECO.summary: Perceived appropriate education level for current or last job (categorical).

p66|ECO.summary: Perceived necessity of specialized training for current or last job (ordinal scale).

p68_1g|ECO.summary: Mother's highest grade level of education completed (numeric).

p71|ECO.summary: Participation in professional training or skills development programs (binary).

p72|ECO.summary: Perceived utility of professional training for labor market improvement (ordinal scale).
